title: NOD-MEG
description: The Natural Object Dataset (NOD) integrates magnetoencephalography (MEG), electroencephalography (EEG), and functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI) data collected from the same subjects viewing naturalistic stimuli from ImageNet. This trimodal neuroimaging resource provides both high spatial resolution (fMRI) and high temporal resolution (MEG/EEG) for investigating object recognition mechanisms. The dataset encompasses diverse naturalistic stimuli and a broad subject pool, facilitating exploration of neural activation patterns across stimuli and subjects. The EEG component is available as a separate dataset (ds005811).
methods_description: MEG and EEG data were collected from subjects viewing naturalistic stimuli (ImageNet images) in multiple sessions and runs, using the same stimulus set as the previously collected NOD-fMRI dataset (ds004496). Raw data were preprocessed and epoched using MNE software (version 1.7.1). Preprocessed full time series and epoch data are provided in BIDS-compliant format with comprehensive trial metadata including stimulus category information and subject responses. The MEG dataset (ds005810) and EEG dataset (ds005811) together form the temporal resolution component of the trimodal NOD resource.
